# sqlalchemy-cubrid

> SQLAlchemy 2.0 dialect for the CUBRID relational database. Provides SQL compilation, type mapping, schema reflection, DDL/DML extensions, Alembic migration support, and PEP 561 typing for Python 3.10+.

sqlalchemy-cubrid enables Python developers to use CUBRID with SQLAlchemy's full feature set — Core, ORM, and Alembic migrations. It supports two driver backends: the C-extension `CUBRIDdb` driver and the pure Python `pycubrid` driver.

Key facts:
- Connection URL: `cubrid://user:password@host:33000/dbname` (C-extension) or `cubrid+pycubrid://user:password@host:33000/dbname` (pure Python)
- CUBRID uses `AUTO_INCREMENT` instead of sequences, `SMALLINT` for booleans, and collection types (`SET`, `MULTISET`, `SEQUENCE`) instead of arrays
- CUBRID does not support `RETURNING`, JSON, or multi-schema — this dialect handles all differences transparently
- DDL is auto-committed (`transactional_ddl = False`)
- Identifier folding is lowercase (not uppercase like SQL standard)
- 6 isolation levels with dual-granularity (class-level + instance-level)
- Install: `pip install sqlalchemy-cubrid` or `pip install "sqlalchemy-cubrid[pycubrid]"` for the pure Python driver

## Docs

- [Connection Guide](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/CONNECTION.md): Connection strings, URL format, driver setup, connection pool tuning
- [Type Mapping](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/TYPES.md): Full type mapping between Python/SQLAlchemy and CUBRID types, including collection types
- [DML Extensions](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/DML_EXTENSIONS.md): ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE, MERGE, REPLACE INTO, GROUP_CONCAT, TRUNCATE
- [Isolation Levels](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/ISOLATION_LEVELS.md): All 6 CUBRID isolation levels and how to configure them
- [Alembic Migrations](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/ALEMBIC.md): Setup, configuration, limitations, batch workarounds
- [Feature Support](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/FEATURE_SUPPORT.md): Feature comparison with MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite
- [ORM Cookbook](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/ORM_COOKBOOK.md): Practical ORM usage examples with CUBRID
- [Driver Compatibility](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/DRIVER_COMPAT.md): Driver versions and known issues
- [Development Guide](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md): Dev setup, testing, Docker, coverage, CI/CD

## Examples

- [Quick Start — Core](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/README.md#quick-start): Basic connection-level usage with `create_engine` and `text()`
- [Quick Start — ORM](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/README.md#orm-session-level): Declarative ORM model definition and CRUD operations
- [CUBRID Cookbook — SQLAlchemy](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-cookbook/tree/main/python/sqlalchemy): Production-ready SQLAlchemy examples with CUBRID
- [CUBRID Cookbook — FastAPI](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-cookbook/tree/main/python/fastapi): FastAPI REST API using sqlalchemy-cubrid

## API Reference

- [Source — dialect.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/sqlalchemy_cubrid/dialect.py): Main dialect class with reflection, connection, isolation levels
- [Source — compiler.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/sqlalchemy_cubrid/compiler.py): SQL/DDL/Type compilers
- [Source — dml.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/sqlalchemy_cubrid/dml.py): Custom DML constructs (insert with ODKU, merge)
- [Source — types.py](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/sqlalchemy_cubrid/types.py): CUBRID type system

## Ecosystem

- [pycubrid](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/pycubrid): Pure Python DB-API 2.0 driver for CUBRID (recommended driver)
- [cubrid-cookbook](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/cubrid-cookbook): Production-ready examples across Python, Node.js, and Go
- [CUBRID](https://www.cubrid.org/): The CUBRID database

## Optional

- [Product Requirements](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/docs/PRD.md): Product requirements document with Example-first Design Philosophy
- [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines
- [Changelog](https://github.com/cubrid-labs/sqlalchemy-cubrid/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Release history
